violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Employees certified in food safety management must register with RIDOH Center for Food Protection as Certified Food Protection Managers. There must be Certified Food Safety Managers on-site for all shifts of operation. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025
Trash and food observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Both the can opener and the ice machine were cleaned and sanitized during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025

The doors to the outside, located next to the walk-in cooler and to the side of the elevator, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/13/2025

The presence of insects is not controlled as evidenced by small flies observed near the 3-bay sink and the handsink. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Pest control must be contacted to increase service as needed to eliminate this issue. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025

Employee was observed not wearing hair restraint. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/07/2025

The floor in the warewashing area and in some areas of the kitchen is in poor repair. Grout has receded well below tile level. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023

Ventilation system for the high temp dishwasher is not sufficient. Steam observed filling the warewashing room when the dishwasher was running. Ventilation for dishwasher must be repaired/increased. New Violation. Correct By: 10/21/2025
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a cart. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

The doors to the outside, located to the side of the walk-in cooler and to the side of the elevator, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024

The presence of pests is not controlled as evidenced by small flies observed near the handsink and in the warewashing area. No food/food equipment observed to be affected. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Pest control has been notified and is working with the facility to eliminate this issue. New Violation. Correct By: 10/28/2024

The 2-door reach-in, the large ice machine, and the dishwasher are in poor repair. This equipment is not currently in use. Service is scheduled today for the dishwasher. 3-bay sink is being utilized to wash, rinse, and sanitize. Paper products are being used for service. Equipment not being used must be properly repaired or removed from the premises. In addition, speed racks, pallet warmer, and dish warmer are not functioning properly and need to be properly repaired or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2024

Flooring in the warewashing area and in some areas of the kitchen is not in good repair. Grout has receded below tile level and some tiles in the warewashing area have lifted off of the floor. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Plan is being discussed to repair this issue. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

The dishwasher failed to wash his/her hands between loading soiled and unloading clean dishes from the dishwasher. Food employees must clean their hands after handling soiled equipment or utensils. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a trash can and a cart. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, utensils, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Can opener and utensils were cleaned and sanitized during inspection. Ice machine has been placed out of service. It is to be drained to be properly cleaned and sanitized prior to next use. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit (rinse temperature of 180 degrees F) was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. 3-bay sink was set up during inspection. 3-bay sink is to be used to sanitize until dishwasher is serviced and reaching correct sanitizing temperature. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Deli meats and cheeses observed not marked with the date of preparation/opening. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods opened/prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Doors to the outside, located to the side of the walk-in cooler and to the side of the elevator, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2024

The presence of insects is not controlled as evidenced by a few small flies observed in the warewashing and handsink areas. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Pest control is to be notified and increased as necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

Employee was observed not wearing a hair restraint. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

2-door reach-in refrigerator and large ice machine are not in good repair. They are not currently in use. Equipment must be maintained in good repair or removed from the premises. In addition, speed racks, pallet warmer, and dish warmer are not functioning properly and must be repaired or replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2024
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2024
Sanitizing bay of the 3-bay sink observed with food debris and trash in it. 3-bay sink must be remain clean. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the ventilation fans in the walk-in cooler have an accumulation of dust, dirt, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

The outside dumpster is not covered. The dumpster is overflowing with cardboard boxes. Many cardboard boxes observed around the dumpster as well. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered and emptied when full in order to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/22/2024

The floor in the warewashing and kitchen areas is in poor repair. Grout has receded below tile level and a couple of tiles in the warewashing area have been lifted off of the floor. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/25/2024

Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate as evidenced by violations observed and cited. In addition, there were no employee present with knowledge to set up the 3-bay sink for sanitizing food contact surfaces and equipment. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023
Employee observed chewing gum on the service line. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a couple of rolling carts. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023
Employee observed touching lip surface of utensils. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/02/2023
The floor in the ware-washing room is in poor repair. Tile grout has receded below tile level. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
Employees were observed changing gloves without washing their hands. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on/changing gloves. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Service call placed during inspection. Dishwasher may be used to wash and rinse dishes/utensils/food equipment. Sanitizing must be done in the 3-bay sink until dishwasher is reaching proper temperature per test strip. Single-use, disposable paper products may be used (if needed) for residents until dishwasher is properly functioning. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The door to the outside, located beside the walk-in cooler, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

The metal wall plate located in the walk-in cooler door area was not in good repair. The bottom section has pulled away from the wall. This area must be properly repaired. New Violation. Correct By: 08/01/2023

Dishwasher not in good repair. A large amount of water splashes from the dishwasher onto the floor and adjacent tables. This water results in flooring issues and creates a source of contamination. Towels have been placed in the area of splash. This area must be protected from splash using materials that are smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent such as a splashguard. New Violation. Correct By: 08/08/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of black/brown residues. Ice machine is to be properly cleaned. New Violation. Correct By: 07/25/2023

The floor in the warewashing area and some areas of the kitchen is in poor repair. Grout has receded below tile level, and a tile under the 3-bay sink has lifted off of the floor. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Flooring must be repaired/replaced using materials that are smooth, durable, easily cleanable, and non-absorbent. New Violation. Correct By: 11/30/2023
